text: Machine learning is a subfield of artificial intelligence. Models are trained on large datasets to learn patterns. The quality of training data significantly impacts model performance.
Deep learning uses neural networks with multiple layers. Backpropagation is the standard algorithm for computing gradients. These gradients are used to update network weights.
The Transformer architecture was introduced in 2017. It relies on self-attention mechanisms rather than recurrence. This allows for better parallelization during training.
Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are designed for grid-like data. They are widely used in computer vision tasks. CNNs learn hierarchical feature representations.
Reinforcement learning involves an agent interacting with an environment. The agent receives rewards or penalties based on its actions. The goal is to maximize cumulative reward.
Neural networks consist of layers of interconnected nodes. Each connection has an associated weight. Training adjusts these weights to minimize error.
Supervised learning requires labeled training examples. The model learns to map inputs to outputs. Classification and regression are common supervised learning tasks.
Unsupervised learning works with unlabeled data. Clustering and dimensionality reduction are typical applications. The model discovers hidden patterns in the data.
Attention mechanisms allow models to focus on relevant parts of the input. Self-attention computes attention weights within a single sequence. This is a key component of Transformers.
Gradient descent is an optimization algorithm used in machine learning. It iteratively adjusts model parameters to minimize a loss function. Learning rate controls the step size.
Overfitting occurs when a model memorizes training data rather than learning general patterns. Regularization techniques help prevent overfitting. Dropout and L2 penalty are common approaches.
Transfer learning leverages knowledge from pre-trained models. This is especially useful when training data is limited. Fine-tuning adapts the model to a new task.
Recurrent neural networks process sequential data. They maintain hidden states that capture temporal dependencies. LSTMs and GRUs are popular RNN variants.
Batch normalization normalizes layer inputs during training. This accelerates training and improves model stability. It reduces internal covariate shift.
Image classification assigns labels to images. CNNs are the dominant architecture for this task. Famous datasets include ImageNet and CIFAR-10.
Natural language processing deals with text and speech. Common tasks include sentiment analysis, translation, and question answering. Transformers revolutionized NLP.
Generative adversarial networks consist of a generator and discriminator. They are trained in a minimax game. GANs can generate realistic synthetic data.
Model evaluation uses metrics like accuracy, precision, and recall. Cross-validation provides robust performance estimates. Test sets must be separate from training data.
Hyperparameter tuning optimizes model configuration. Grid search and random search are common methods. Bayesian optimization is more efficient for expensive evaluations.
Ensemble methods combine multiple models to improve performance. Bagging and boosting are popular techniques. Random forests are a widely used ensemble approach.
